messages.ua: The Verkhovna Rada adopted a statement on the need to hold Russia’s leadership accountable for crimes in Ukraine, in particular to enforce the International Criminal Court’s decision to arrest Vladimir Putin and Maria Lvova-Belova.
messages.ru: The ICC, not recognized by Russia, issued a warrant for the “arrest” of Putin and Lvova-Belova.
messages.neutral: The International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants for Vladimir Putin and Maria Lvova-Belova.

messages.ua: On February 23, the United Nations, by an overwhelming majority, voted to demand that Russia “immediately” and “unconditionally” withdraw its troops from Ukraine. The document submitted by Kyiv for the first anniversary of the full-scale war calls for establishing a “just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter.”
messages.ru: UN, Feb 23 — RIA Novosti. The UN General Assembly adopted an anti-Russian resolution prepared by Western countries on the “principles underlying a comprehensive, just and lasting peace in Ukraine,” a RIA Novosti correspondent reports. 141 states voted for the resolution, seven voted against, and 32 abstained. In addition, the text calls on Moscow to immediately stop strikes on Ukraine’s critical infrastructure and warns against attacks on “civilian objects, including residential buildings, schools and hospitals.” There are no similar calls addressed to Kyiv in the text. It also says not a word about the need to stop shelling of Donbas by the Armed Forces of Ukraine. This is already the sixth anti-Russian resolution adopted by the General Assembly since the start of the special operation. Earlier, Russia’s permanent representative to the UN, Vasily Nebenzya, told RIA Novosti that the conditions embedded in the “peace” initiatives put forward by Kyiv are obviously unacceptable.
messages.neutral: On February 23, the UN General Assembly adopted a resolution calling for a comprehensive, just, and lasting peace in Ukraine and demanding that Russia immediately withdraw its troops. The resolution was supported by 141 countries, with seven voting against and 32 abstaining.

messages.ua: We must stop Putin as strongly as possible, the U.S. reacted to the decision to put nuclear forces on alert.
messages.ru: Putin ordered the deterrence forces to be put on a special combat duty regime.
messages.neutral: Vladimir Putin ordered Russia’s nuclear forces to be put on alert.

messages.ua: The order by the president of the aggressor state, Vladimir Putin, to put Russia’s deterrence forces, which include nuclear weapons, on heightened combat readiness is an unacceptable escalation. This was stated by U.S. Ambassador to the UN Linda Thomas-Greenfield, as reported by “European Pravda” citing Reuters.
messages.ru: "Senior officials of leading NATO countries allow themselves aggressive statements toward our country, therefore I order the minister of defense and the chief of the General Staff to transfer the deterrence forces of the Russian army to a special combat duty regime," Putin said (as quoted by TASS).
messages.neutral: Vladimir Putin ordered Russia’s deterrent forces, including nuclear-capable units, to be placed on high alert.

messages.ua: Zelenskyy emphasized that despite statements by the Russian side, shelling of Ukrainian cities continues, and that “there is no reason to trust them without concrete results.”
messages.ru: Following the talks in Istanbul, Russia announced a reduction in military activity in the Kyiv and Chernihiv directions. And Ukraine handed over its proposals for a peace treaty. Now the main question is whether Moscow will accept them.
messages.neutral: After talks in Istanbul, Russia announced a reduction of military activity near Kyiv and Chernihiv, but no major advance was made toward a peace agreement.

messages.ua: "Nearly 85% of all the bodies we recover from mass graves or individual burials in gardens, parks, squares, courtyards have bullet holes. That is, a deliberate, intentional killing took place in Bucha," Fedoruk said on the air of the national TV marathon.
messages.ru: Even a year later, the Kremlin claims that the killings in Bucha are fake. None of this happened.
messages.neutral: Reports and investigations have documented numerous civilian deaths in Bucha, with differing claims about the circumstances and responsibility.

messages.ua: Russia confirmed: the cruiser “Moskva” sank.
messages.ru: The cruiser “Moskva” sank while being towed.
messages.neutral: The Russian cruiser Moskva sank.

messages.ua: The defenders of Mariupol at Azovstal completed their mission - the command ordered them to preserve their lives.
messages.ru: The last defenders of Azovstal surrendered in Mariupol.
messages.neutral: The remaining Ukrainian forces at the Azovstal plant in Mariupol left their positions.

messages.ua: Ukraine’s Armed Forces withdrew from Sievierodonetsk to prepared positions.
messages.ru: Defense Ministry: allied forces established full control over Sievierodonetsk.
messages.neutral: Control over Severodonetsk shifted from Ukraine to Russia.

messages.ua: Russia struck a shopping mall in Kremenchuk: many dead and wounded.
messages.ru: Defense Ministry: the strike was carried out on an arms depot in Kremenchuk.
messages.neutral: A Russian missile strike occurred in Kremenchuk, causing explosions and casualties.

messages.ua: Ukraine’s Armed Forces liberated Snake Island.
messages.ru: Troops left Snake Island as a “gesture of goodwill.”
messages.neutral: Russian forces left Snake Island, and Ukrainian forces regained control of it.

messages.ua: Ukraine signed the “grain initiative” in Istanbul.
messages.ru: Documents on exporting grain were signed in Istanbul.
messages.neutral: Agreements on grain exports were signed in Istanbul.

messages.ua: Fighting continues; defenders control part of the facilities / advances on the flanks.
messages.ru: Russian forces fully “liberated” Artemovsk.
messages.neutral: Heavy fighting took place around Artemovsk.

messages.ua: On the night of June 6, Russian troops blew up the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station. Evacuation was announced for about 80 settlements.
messages.ru: RIA Novosti publishes a video of the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station destroyed by Ukrainian troops. The clip shows the destroyed spillway dam. The building of the machine hall split into two parts, one of which partly went under water.
messages.neutral: An explosion destroyed parts of the Kakhovka hydroelectric power station, causing severe damage to the dam and flooding in nearby settlements.

messages.ua: On the night of November 19, the Armed Forces of Ukraine struck a military facility in Bryansk region with ATACMS missiles, Russia’s Defense Ministry said. The statement claims five ATACMS missiles were shot down… and one was damaged. Debris fell and the fire was promptly extinguished. There were no casualties or destruction as a result of the attack, it said.
messages.ru: Last night, the Armed Forces of Ukraine struck Bryansk region with six ATACMS ballistic missiles; air defense systems repelled the attack, the Defense Ministry reported. S-400 and Pantsir crews shot down five missiles and damaged the sixth. There were no casualties or destruction, the ministry emphasized.
messages.neutral: On the night of November 19, Ukrainian forces launched ATACMS missiles at the Bryansk region. No casualties or significant damage were reported.

messages.ua: Deputy Defense Minister Oleksandr Havryliuk reported that additional deliveries of F-16, Mirage, and Gripen aircraft are expected.
messages.ru: The Kyiv regime will additionally receive F-16 aircraft, French Mirage and Swedish Gripen jets, Deputy Defense Minister Havryliuk reported.
messages.neutral: Ukraine expects additional deliveries of F-16, Mirage, and Gripen fighter aircraft.

messages.ua: On the night of September 23, [Ukrainian] drones carried out a massive attack on several regions of Russia. One feature of this attack was the use of unknown balloons that confused Russian air defense.
messages.ru: In the repelled drone attack on Russian regions last night, balloons were used en masse, RBC sources said. A feature of the attack was the large number of balloons.
messages.neutral: On the night of September 23, drones and balloons were reportedly used in an attack on several regions of Russia, with air defenses responding to the incident.

messages.ua: In occupied Nova Kakhovka in Kherson region, on October 1, an alleged Ukrainian drone carried out an attack in which the “head of the Council of Deputies,” collaborator Volodymyr Leontiev, was killed.
messages.ru: The Russian army shot down 20 UAF drones over Russian territory on October 1. As a result of a drone attack in Kherson region, the chairman of the Council of Deputies of Nova Kakhovka, Vladimir Leontiev, was killed, Russian authorities report.
messages.neutral: On October 1, an attack involving drones occurred in Nova Kakhovka, Kherson region, resulting in the death of local official Volodymyr Leontiev.

messages.ua: Ukrainian drones destroyed a bridge in Russia’s Belgorod region that the Russian army used for logistics.
messages.ru: Ukrainian drones attacked and destroyed a bridge in Russia’s Belgorod region that was used by the Russian army, Ukraine’s military intelligence (GUR) reports.
messages.neutral: Ukrainian drones destroyed a bridge in Russia’s Belgorod region that was used by the Russian army.

messages.ua: As a result of a GUR operation, an ammunition depot and Russian soldiers whom the aggressor state had redeployed to the Zaporizhzhia front were destroyed.
messages.ru: As a result of an operation by the Main Intelligence Directorate, an ammunition depot and Russian servicemen redeployed to the Zaporizhzhia front were destroyed.
messages.neutral: An operation by Ukraine’s Main Intelligence Directorate destroyed an ammunition depot and Russian troops positioned on the Zaporizhzhia Front.

messages.ua: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs accused Iran of involvement in terrorist acts that the Russian Federation is committing against Ukraine’s civilian population and critical infrastructure, using Iranian-made combat drones.
messages.ru: Peskov said the Kremlin has no information about Iranian drones.
messages.neutral: Iran may have supplied combat drones to Russia.

messages.ua: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs accused Iran of involvement in terrorist acts that the Russian Federation is committing against Ukraine’s civilian population and critical infrastructure, using Iranian-made combat drones.
messages.ru: Kyiv and Washington reported the use of Iranian-made drones in Ukraine. The EU launched an investigation on this and threatened Iran with sanctions. Moscow and Tehran have repeatedly denied supplying combat UAVs.
messages.neutral: Iran may have supplied combat drones to Russia.

messages.ua: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called Russian President Vladimir Putin’s initiative for a three-day ceasefire until May 9 a theatrical production. He rejected Putin’s proposal.
messages.ru: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy rejected Russian leader Vladimir Putin’s proposal for a three-day ceasefire until May 9, calling it a “theatrical production.” Instead, he said a 30-day ceasefire should be implemented.
messages.neutral: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy rejected Russian President Vladimir Putin’s proposal for a three-day ceasefire until May 9.

messages.ua: The Kremlin lied again: the Center for Countering Disinformation debunked Putin’s fake statement about Chasiv Yar.
messages.ru: Putin said that Chasiv Yar was taken several days ago.
messages.neutral: There were claims that Russian forces had captured Chasov Yar, though this had not been independently verified.

messages.ua: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called the situation at the Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant critical after it was left without electricity due to Russian shelling.
messages.ru: It’s foolish to blame Russia for attacks on the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant.
messages.neutral: The Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant experienced power outages.

messages.ua: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that the Russians would do everything possible to prevent a leaders-level meeting between Ukraine, the United States, and Russia from taking place.
messages.ru: Lavrov said that Zelenskyy answered “no” to all of Trump’s proposals.
messages.neutral: Zelenskyy and Russian representatives stated that peace negotiations have not advanced.

messages.ua: Through Crimea, Russia returned to the old imperial habit of taking what is precious. Dear friends, we must remember what Crimea is. We must act so that Russia knows: we remember, we do not forgive. We will fight for international law, and we will win.
messages.ru: Crimea and Russia’s new subjects never belonged to Ukraine by right. Ukraine emerged as a state as a result of the lawlessness of the collapse of the USSR, lacks legitimacy, and is a failed project. Similarly, this is not about “returning” but about restoring the legal status of the territories.
messages.neutral: Crimea and several other regions remain disputed between Russia and Ukraine, with both sides asserting sovereignty over them.

messages.ua: On September 1 in Foros, the “Foros” sanatorium and a school were damaged. Aksyonov also claimed there were fatalities as a result of the attack, with the number being clarified, and that 15 people were injured.
messages.ru: Crimea will learn lessons from the attack by UAF drones on the settlement of Foros, Konstantinov said.
messages.neutral: On September 1, an attack in Foros, Crimea, damaged buildings and caused casualties.

messages.ua: The enemy struck Kharkiv with a guided aerial bomb (KAB) — an explosion was heard in the city, the mayor confirmed. “Kharkiv is under a combined attack… the Kyivskyi district has been hit by KABs and missiles.”
messages.ru: Fifteen explosions were heard in Kharkiv, and later one more. A Kharkiv TV channel reported a series of explosions. Later the number was updated to 16.
messages.neutral: Kharkiv experienced multiple explosions during a combined attack.

messages.ua: On Wednesday, August 13, plumes of black smoke rose over the Russian city of Belgorod near a military airfield. According to reports, the enemy facility was struck by a drone; judging by the smoke, fuels and lubricants caught fire as a result of the strike.
messages.ru: UAF drone attacks on Belgorod continue. A civilian was wounded. As a result of a drone detonation in the courtyard of an apartment building, a man was injured.
messages.neutral: Drone attacks were reported in the Russian city of Belgorod on August 13.

messages.ua: President Zelenskyy said Ukraine carried out a strike on Kursk region. Russia deployed about 12,000 North Korean soldiers there. About 4,000 DPRK soldiers have already been eliminated.
messages.ru: The number of people injured in the Ukrainian drone raid on a beach in Kursk has risen to ten. Three people were killed.
messages.neutral: Ukrainian drone strikes were reported in Russia’s Kursk region, resulting in casualties.

messages.ua: In Odesa on October 1, an explosion was heard during an air raid alert.
messages.ru: Another explosion thundered in Odesa in southern Ukraine on Tuesday amid an air raid alert, Ukrainian TV channel “Suspilne” reports.
messages.neutral: Explosions were reported in Odessa, southern Ukraine, on October 1 during an air raid alert.

messages.ua: On February 23, Ukrainian troops shot down a Russian A-50 airborne early warning aircraft over the Sea of Azov.
messages.ru: Russian presidential press secretary Dmitry Peskov said the Kremlin has no information that the Armed Forces of Ukraine shot down two Russian army aircraft — an A-50 and an Il-22.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50 aircraft was reportedly shot down over the Sea of Azov on February 23.

messages.ua: Ukraine attacked Russia using at least 84 unmanned aerial vehicles, 34 of which were aimed at Moscow.
messages.ru: On the morning of November 10, the Armed Forces of Ukraine attempted to attack the capital region with a record number of drones. Air defense intercepted 34 drones flying toward Moscow.
messages.neutral: On November 10, Ukraine launched a large-scale drone attack on Russia.

messages.ua: Brazil plans on Friday, September 27, to discuss in New York with 20 Global South countries a joint six-point “peace plan” with China to end the Russia-Ukraine war.
messages.ru: China and Brazil want to revive a plan to resolve the Ukrainian conflict.
messages.neutral: China and Brazil announced plans to discuss a joint peace initiative aimed at resolving the conflict between Russia and Ukraine.

messages.ua: As a result of Ukrainian missiles being shot down by Russian air defense systems, vacationers were killed and wounded.
messages.ru: According to the Sevastopol government, four civilians were killed in a terrorist attack — two adults and two children.
messages.neutral: Civilian casualties were reported in Sevastopol following a Ukrainian attack and air defense response.

messages.ua: According to reports, on March 15 the Russian army carried out a missile strike on Odesa. It is currently known that 21 people were killed and more than 70 were injured.
messages.ru: According to Mriya in Odessa, about 100 foreign mercenaries were killed.
messages.neutral: A missile strike hit Odessa on March 15, resulting in multiple deaths and injuries.

messages.ua: On the night of August 26, the Russian army carried out a combined attack across the country. In Zhytomyr region, residential buildings were hit; a woman was killed and four people were injured.
messages.ru: Explosions occurred in the city of Zhytomyr, the Ukrainian TV channel “Strana” reported.
messages.neutral: On the night of August 26, explosions occurred in the city of Zhytomyr.

messages.ua: Zelenskyy thanked the United States for its support, noting that the plant’s workers saved millions of Ukrainians, and emphasized that here he feels how the democratic world can win.
messages.ru: Congresswoman Taylor Greene said the U.S. should not support Kyiv with weapons but negotiate with Russia.
messages.neutral: Opinions in the U.S. differ over the scale and form of assistance provided to Ukraine.

messages.ua: The Defense Ministry of the aggressor country blamed Ukraine for the plane crash. Russians claim 65 Ukrainian prisoners of war were killed.
messages.ru: The Defense Ministry said Kyiv committed a terrorist act by shooting down an Il-76 aircraft that was carrying out a humanitarian mission — there were 65 UAF prisoners on board.
messages.neutral: A Russian Il-76 aircraft carrying Ukrainian prisoners of war crashed.

messages.ua: Ukrainian troops withdrew from Avdiivka to avoid encirclement.
messages.ru: The Russian Armed Forces fully took control of Avdiivka in the DPR, the Defense Ministry reported.
messages.neutral: Ukrainian troops withdrew from Avdiivka as Russian forces took control.

messages.ua: In Kharkiv region, the Russian occupation army almost completely captured the village of Kolisnykivka and made gains near Lyman Pershyi and Synkivka.
messages.ru: The Russian army is regaining positions in Kharkiv region; nine settlements and more than 200 sq. km of land have been liberated.
messages.neutral: Russian forces advanced in the Kharkiv region, capturing several settlements.

messages.ua: Another successful prisoner exchange. We brought 50 of our warriors home.
messages.ru: On January 8, 50 Russian servicemen were returned from territory controlled by the Kyiv regime.
messages.neutral: On January 8, a prisoner exchange took place, with 50 prisoners of war returned on each side.

messages.ua: The Black Sea Grain Initiative was unilaterally terminated by Russia on July 17. Moscow’s desire to use grain as a weapon became its strategy for global-scale blackmail.
messages.ru: On July 13, Putin said that nothing had been done for Russia under the grain deal, but that Moscow had repeatedly extended the document in good faith. He warned that Russia was considering suspending participation.
messages.neutral: Russia ended its participation in the Black Sea Grain Initiative on July 17, after previously warning it might suspend involvement.

messages.ua: Russia unilaterally terminated the Black Sea Grain Initiative on July 17 after effectively blocking it for several months.
messages.ru: The period for which the grain deal was extended expires on July 17.
messages.neutral: The Black Sea Grain Initiative ended on July 17, 2023

messages.ua: This morning, October 8, a powerful explosion occurred on the Crimean Bridge, triggering a major fire and destruction. Panic spread among residents of the occupied peninsula, while Russian authorities began searching for those responsible.
messages.ru: A truck was blown up on the Crimean Bridge, causing two roadway spans to partially collapse. According to preliminary data, three people were killed. Details of the incident.
messages.neutral: On October 8, an explosion occurred on the Crimean Bridge.

messages.ua: The share of deliveries is only 11.6% to Africa, 48.2% to Asia, and 40.2% to Europe. Russia is trying to discredit the “grain initiative.”
messages.ru: According to Vladimir Putin, almost all the grain exported from Ukrainian ports ended up in European countries.
messages.neutral: Russia claimed that most Ukrainian grain exports went to Europe, while Ukraine stated that the shipments were distributed among Africa, Asia, and Europe.

messages.ua: Why does Putin need pseudo-referendums in occupied territories and what fate awaits Ukrainians who take Russian passports — read more in the RBC-Ukraine material.
messages.ru: Voting will last five days, including the first four outside polling stations. Russian authorities promised to recognize the referendum results.
messages.neutral: Russia organized referendums in multiple regions, scheduled to last five days.

messages.ua: As a result of Russians blowing up the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station on June 6, 2023, 31 people died in government-controlled territory.
messages.ru: On June 6, the dam of the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station, located upstream of Kherson on the Dnipro River, collapsed. Moscow called the dam’s destruction sabotage by Kyiv; Ukrainian authorities accused Russia of blowing it up. Both sides announced evacuations from flooded areas.
messages.neutral: On June 6, 2023, an explosion occurred at the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station, resulting in casualties, with Russia and Ukraine blaming each other for the incident.

messages.ua: Elon Musk accused Zelenskyy of the death of an American journalist.
messages.ru: Musk accused Zelenskyy of killing the American blogger Lira.
messages.neutral: Elon Musk accused President Volodymyr Zelensky of involvement in the death of an American journalist.

messages.ua: No terrorist in the world has allowed themselves such a thing: Zelenskyy called the situation at the Russian-seized Zaporizhzhia NPP critical.
messages.ru: Zelenskyy: the situation at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant is critical.
messages.neutral: President Volodymyr Zelensky stated that the situation at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant is critical.

messages.ua: THE AIR FORCE REPELLED A MASSIVE MISSILE STRIKE ON UKRAINE: MORE THAN 60 OUT OF OVER 70 OCCUPIERS’ MISSILES WERE SHOT DOWN. On December 5, Russian occupiers carried out a massive missile strike on critical infrastructure facilities in Ukraine. In total, more than 70 missiles were launched. According to preliminary information, launches of 38 Kh-101/Kh-555 cruise missiles were carried out from eight Tu-95MS strategic bomber aircraft from the Caspian Sea area and Volgodonsk in Russia’s Rostov region. The enemy also struck with 22 Kalibr cruise missiles from Black Sea Fleet ships. In addition, long-range Tu-22M3 bombers attacked Ukraine from the Black Sea with three Kh-22 cruise missiles, and Su-35 fighters with six Kh-59 guided air missiles and one Kh-31P. Anti-aircraft missile units, aviation, mobile fire groups of the Air Force of the Armed Forces of Ukraine and other components of the Defense Forces were involved in repelling the air attack. In total, more than 60 enemy missiles were shot down!
messages.ru: On December 5, Russian troops carried out a massive strike with long-range high-precision air- and sea-launched weapons against the military command system, defense industry facilities and associated energy facilities of Ukraine. The goal of the strike was achieved. All designated targets were hit. As a result of the strike, production and repair of Ukrainian weapons at defense industry enterprises was halted, and rail transport of military equipment for the UAF, including foreign-made, was disrupted.
messages.neutral: On December 5, Russia launched a large-scale missile attack on targets in Ukraine, with both sides reporting differing outcomes of the strike.

messages.ua: Ukrainian troops today, September 22, attacked the occupiers’ Black Sea Fleet headquarters in Sevastopol.
messages.ru: The strike by the Armed Forces of Ukraine (UAF) on the Black Sea Fleet headquarters in Sevastopol is a military challenge. This was stated to RIA Novosti by Crimea’s permanent representative to the Russian president, Georgy Muradov.
messages.neutral: On September 22, Ukrainian forces attacked the headquarters of the Russian Black Sea Fleet in Sevastopol.

messages.ua: Ukraine used ATACMS missiles provided by the U.S. for the first time to strike airfields in Berdiansk and Luhansk. These attacks are linked to Russia’s ability both to defend and to advance at the front.
messages.ru: The Armed Forces of Ukraine (UAF) received the first batch of Army Tactical Missile System (ATACMS) tactical ballistic missiles. According to The Wall Street Journal, it was a small number of missiles that Washington secretly delivered to Kyiv in recent days.
messages.neutral: Ukraine received and used U.S.-supplied ATACMS missiles for the first time, striking several targets.

messages.ua: British intelligence indicates that over the past two weeks Russian troops have significantly intensified their offensive toward Kupyansk-Lyman, but it is unlikely the occupiers will achieve a major operational breakthrough there.
messages.ru: Russian troops continue to strike UAF positions and repel the enemy’s attempts at counteroffensives in various directions, according to the Russian Defense Ministry’s November 1 briefing on the course of the special military operation.
messages.neutral: Russian forces intensified operations in the Kupyansk-Liman area, carrying out ongoing attacks.

messages.ua: The enemy is conducting offensive actions in the east of our country to establish full control over the territory of Donetsk and Luhansk regions and to maintain a land corridor between these territories and occupied Crimea.
messages.ru: Briefing of the Russian Defense Ministry (06.05.2022). The Armed Forces of the Russian Federation continue the special military operation in Ukraine. High-precision air-launched missiles destroyed a large ammunition depot of a Ukrainian troop grouping located on the territory of the Energomashspetsstal plant in the city of Kramatorsk. In the area of the settlement of Novotavrychne in Zaporizhzhia region, a Tochka-U tactical missile system of Ukrainian troops was destroyed.
messages.neutral: Fighting continued in eastern Ukraine as Russian forces conducted strikes in the Donetsk and Luhansk regions and nearby areas.

messages.ua: Russian President Vladimir Putin instructed the leadership of Russia’s Defense Ministry and General Staff to transfer the deterrence forces in the Russian army to a special duty regime.
messages.ru: On February 27, 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered the Defense Ministry to transfer the deterrence forces to a special duty regime. He noted that Western countries were “taking not only unfriendly actions against our country in the economic sphere, but senior officials of leading NATO countries are making aggressive statements toward our country.”
messages.neutral: On February 27, 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered the country’s deterrent forces to be placed on special alert.

messages.ua: On March 9, 2022, Russian troops committed one of the most terrible crimes against humanity — they dropped a bomb on a children’s hospital and a maternity hospital in the center of Mariupol.
messages.ru: The situation in Ukraine and Donbas has turned into an information war, with fakes becoming more sophisticated every day. Recently the world was shocked by news of the shelling of a hospital in Mariupol, where a maternity ward and children’s department are located, but both the report and the photos from the “scene” turned out to be false.
messages.neutral: An explosion occurred at a maternity and children’s hospital in Mariupol on March 9, 2022, with conflicting reports about the cause.

messages.ua: The enemy carried out a missile strike on a training ground near Lviv. According to preliminary data, air defense worked. The occupiers carried out an airstrike on the International Peacekeeping and Security Center (Yavoriv training ground).
messages.ru: On March 13, 2022, at the very beginning of the special military operation, the Yavoriv training ground was hit by the first Russian missile strike. As a result of the strike, about 300 UAF fighters and an unspecified number of foreign mercenaries — including instructors — were killed. The Kyiv regime claimed foreigners left in February, but NATO officers were also present there.
messages.neutral: Russia launched a missile strike on the International Peacekeeping and Security Center (Yavoriv Training Ground) near Lviv on March 13, 2022.

messages.ua: Russian terrorists bombed the Mariupol Drama Theater and killed dozens of citizens.
messages.ru: Ukrainian volunteers and soldiers brought explosives into the Mariupol Drama Theater and accidentally detonated them.
messages.neutral: An explosion occurred at the Mariupol Drama Theater, resulting in multiple civilian deaths.

messages.ua: Statement of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine regarding the shelling by Russians of a detention camp with our prisoners near Olenivka. According to the Commander of Missile Forces and Artillery of the Army Command of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, the Armed Forces of Ukraine did not carry out any missile or artillery strikes in the area of the settlement of Olenivka. Today alone, we lost 53 defenders there.
messages.ru: Last night, the Kyiv regime deliberately carried out a bloody provocation. A missile strike was carried out on a detention facility in the area of the settlement of Yelenovka, where Ukrainian POWs were held, including fighters of the “Azov” formation, using the American HIMARS multiple launch rocket system. 40 Ukrainian POWs were killed and 75 were wounded.
messages.neutral: An attack occurred at a detention facility near Olenivka where Ukrainian prisoners of war were held, resulting in numerous deaths and injuries.

messages.ua: General Staff: Behind every so-called “gesture of goodwill” by the enemy stand enormous efforts by our soldiers. Deputy Chief of the Main Operational Directorate of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, Brigadier General Oleksii Hromov: In the Kherson direction, the Defense Forces of Ukraine destroyed the enemy’s logistics routes and supply system, disrupted its troop command-and-control system, leaving the enemy with no choice but to flee.
messages.ru: The enemy plans to destroy the dam at the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station and flood territories downstream along the Dnipro, which poses a great threat to the Russian army. Sergey Surovikin stated that Russian forces are countering all attempts by the UAF to advance in this direction, and that the enemy has suffered heavy losses.
messages.neutral: Fighting continued in the Kherson region near the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station, with both sides reporting military actions and damage in the area.

messages.ua: Just as the enemy retreated from Kyiv, from Kharkiv region, left Snake Island, the likely withdrawal from Kherson is the result of active actions by Ukrainian forces. At present, it is impossible to either refute or confirm information about the so-called withdrawal of Russian troops from Kherson. We continue conducting an offensive operation according to our plan.
messages.ru: The enemy plans to destroy the dam at the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station and flood territories downstream along the Dnipro, which poses a great threat to the Russian army. Sergey Surovikin stated that Russian forces are countering all attempts by the UAF to advance in this direction, and that the enemy has suffered heavy losses.
messages.neutral: Military operations continued around the Kherson region and the Kakhovka Hydroelectric Power Station.

messages.ua: Claims by Russian occupiers that they captured the center of Lysychansk in Luhansk region are fake. However, the situation in the city is threatening; very heavy fighting is ongoing for it.
messages.ru: The head of the Luhansk regional administration, Serhiy Haidai, said that in the near future Ukrainian troops will have to leave Sievierodonetsk “because holding positions in smashed positions makes no sense: the number of dead is growing.” According to him, in the city, which Russian forces have been shelling daily for four months, 90% of buildings have been destroyed or damaged.
messages.neutral: Heavy fighting took place in the Luhansk region, particularly around Lysychansk and Severodonetsk.

messages.ua: For everyone in Ukraine who deals with European integration and reforms, June 17 was a very good day. On this front, Ukraine achieved a victory: the European Commission confirmed that our state deserves candidate status for EU membership.
messages.ru: Candidate status for EU membership for Ukraine and Moldova is the final act of reformatting the continent. The era of buffer zones is ending, Roman Goncharenko believes. This is that rare case when pathos is appropriate. The EU decision to grant candidate status to Ukraine and Moldova is a historic, epochal event not only for the two former Soviet republics but also for Brussels. It is a watershed.
messages.neutral: The European Union granted Ukraine and Moldova candidate status for EU membership.

messages.ua: 86 days of Mariupol. A chronicle of courage, defense, and the tragedy of the city, where Russia committed the gravest war crimes.
messages.ru: Mariupol is Russian. Results of the first three months of the military operation.
messages.neutral: Mariupol experienced intense fighting, after which the city came under Russian control.

messages.ua: Ukraine’s Armed Forces achieved the impossible: no one in history had ever shot down an early warning radar aircraft, Defense Express reports. Previously, destroying such an aircraft was considered impossible, because these planes try to avoid the front line and stay as far as possible from the enemy’s air defense zone. In addition, they are protected from fighter attacks by an escort, and the surface-to-air missile system itself can guide them to enemy aircraft. Russia’s Aerospace Forces have a shortage of A-50 aircraft. In total, “on paper” there are only nine aircraft, three of which are Soviet-era A-50s, and six are modernized A-50Us, one of which was damaged in Belarus. The real number that are flightworthy is unknown. Therefore, the destruction of such an aircraft means more than significant losses for the enemy and creates a gap in its air defense and aviation command-and-control system.
messages.ru: Dmitry Peskov said the Kremlin has no information about Russian aircraft shot down over the Sea of Azov. The UAF claims that Ukraine shot down an Il-22M and an A-50; Russian war correspondents say the aircraft may have been shot down by friendly fire.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50 and an Il-22 aircraft were reportedly shot down over the Sea of Azov, with Ukraine claiming responsibility and Russian sources suggesting possible friendly fire.

messages.ua: Ukraine’s Armed Forces achieved the impossible: no one in history had ever shot down an early warning radar aircraft, Defense Express reports. Previously, destroying such an aircraft was considered impossible, because these planes try to avoid the front line and stay as far as possible from the enemy’s air defense zone. In addition, they are protected from fighter attacks by an escort, and the surface-to-air missile system itself can guide them to enemy aircraft. Russia’s Aerospace Forces have a shortage of A-50 aircraft.
messages.ru: The Russian president’s press secretary commented on reports about two aircraft — an A-50 and an Il-22 of the Russian Aerospace Forces — allegedly being shot down over the Sea of Azov. Peskov advised clarifying information about the course of the special military operation with the Defense Ministry. The Defense Ministry has not commented on the aircraft reports so far. The first reports about the aircraft losses appeared among Russian war correspondents.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50 and an Il-22 aircraft were reportedly shot down over the Sea of Azov.

messages.ua: The statement notes the following: Under the agreements, the security of our Defenders was to be ensured by the Russian side. At the same time, the Ukrainian side was not informed of the need to ensure the safety of the airspace in the area of the city of Belgorod during a specified period of time, as had been done repeatedly in the past. Ukraine was not informed about the number of transport vehicles, routes, and delivery formats for the prisoners. This may indicate deliberate actions by Russia aimed at creating a threat to the life and safety of the prisoners. Landing a transport aircraft within a 30-km zone of active hostilities cannot be safe and in any case must be discussed by both sides, because otherwise it endangers the entire exchange process. Hours earlier, the General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine reported the following: The Armed Forces of Ukraine will continue taking measures to destroy delivery means and control the airspace to eliminate the terrorist threat, including in the Belgorod-Kharkiv direction.
messages.ru: Operational services are checking information about the crash of an Il-76 military transport aircraft in Belgorod region. Preliminary reports suggest the aircraft may have crashed near the hamlet of Krivoye. “An investigative team and emergency services are working on site. I changed my work schedule and went to the area. All details later,” Governor Gladkov wrote.
messages.neutral: A Russian Il-76 military transport plane crashed near the village of Krivoye in the Belgorod region, and emergency services are investigating the incident.

messages.ua: The statement notes the following: Under the agreements, the security of our Defenders was to be ensured by the Russian side. At the same time, the Ukrainian side was not informed of the need to ensure the safety of the airspace in the area of the city of Belgorod during a specified period of time, as had been done repeatedly in the past. Ukraine was not informed about the number of transport vehicles, routes, and delivery formats for the prisoners. This may indicate deliberate actions by Russia aimed at creating a threat to the life and safety of the prisoners. Landing a transport aircraft within a 30-km zone of active hostilities cannot be safe and in any case must be discussed by both sides, because otherwise it endangers the entire exchange process. Hours earlier, the General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine reported the following: The Armed Forces of Ukraine will continue taking measures to destroy delivery means and control the airspace to eliminate the terrorist threat, including in the Belgorod-Kharkiv direction.
messages.ru: There were 65 captured UAF servicemen on board the downed Il-76, being transported to Belgorod region for an exchange, as well as 6 crew members and 3 escorts (74 people total). This was reported by the Defense Ministry.
messages.neutral: A Russian Il-76 transport plane carrying prisoners of war crashed in the Belgorod region.

messages.ua: DiCarlo called for avoiding further escalation and for continuing prisoner exchanges. It is clear only that the incident occurred in the context of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and the ongoing war. To avoid further escalation, we call on all stakeholders to refrain from actions, rhetoric, or accusations that could further inflame an already dangerous conflict. We call on the sides to continue exchanging prisoners of war.
messages.ru: Today, both flight recorders of the Il-76 were delivered to Moscow to a military laboratory; according to specialists, their condition is suitable for decoding. The UN Security Council held a meeting on the Il-76 crash. Ukrainian representatives said Kyiv had not been informed about the type of transport Russia used to move POWs.
messages.neutral: The prisoner exchange between Ukraine and Russia has not yet occurred amid ongoing investigations and diplomatic discussions.

messages.ua: On January 24, they planned to exchange 65 Ukrainian defenders whose names were published by Russian propagandists after the Il-76 crash — the Coordination Headquarters for the Treatment of Prisoners of War said. However, relatives of fighters from the mentioned list, after reviewing video materials shown by Russian propagandists allegedly from the crash site, have not yet found signs in the published body fragments indicating their relatives. The GUR still does not know for sure whether these prisoners were on board at the time of the crash and adds that Russia’s version raises many questions.
messages.ru: “Baza” publishes a list of three escorts who flew together with Ukrainian POWs and died in the Il-76 plane crash in Belgorod region: Gavrish I. — warrant officer; Shashkov I. — private; Kulikov N. — private. The servicemen were in the Il-76 together with 65 prisoners and six crew members. Blogger Yuri Podolyaka also wrote that representatives of the Russian Defense Ministry’s negotiating group — State Duma deputy Shamsail Saraliev and General Sergey Egorov — were supposed to be on the plane. The Investigative Committee said the Il-76 near Belgorod was shot down by a missile from Kharkiv region. Allegedly, several Patriot PAC-3 missiles were launched at the aircraft.
messages.neutral: A Russian Il-76 transport plane crashed in the Belgorod region on January 24 during a planned prisoner exchange.

messages.ua: Ukrainian troops left Avdiivka to avoid encirclement and withdrew to new defensive lines, Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said. “Based on the operational situation around Avdiivka, in order to avoid encirclement and preserve the lives and health of servicemen, I decided to withdraw our units from the city and transition to defense on more advantageous lines,” Syrskyi said.
messages.ru: The liberation of Avdiivka is necessary to push the front line away from Donetsk, and, most importantly, to protect it from terrorist attacks by the criminal Kyiv regime. Information about the troop advance was not made public until the complete defeat and capture of the city. The “Center” group’s troops continue offensive operations to liberate the Donetsk People’s Republic from Ukrainian nationalists.
messages.neutral: Russian forces took control of Avdiivka as Ukrainian troops withdrew from the city.

messages.ua: War is about who killed whom and how many. In that sense, Avdiivka was very advantageous for us, said Yevhen Dykyi, former commander of an assault company of the Aidar battalion. He understands that saying this is absolutely inhumane because we also lost many people there. But by the orcs’ calculations, in the battles for Avdiivka they deployed more fighters than for Bakhmut.
messages.ru: Today in the Kremlin, Defense Minister S.K. Shoigu reported to the Supreme Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation on the full capture of the city of Avdiivka of the Donetsk People’s Republic, which is a defensive hub of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, under the control of the “Center” group of forces under the command of Colonel General Andrei Mordvichev.
messages.neutral: Heavy fighting took place in Avdiivka, resulting in significant losses on both sides.

messages.ua: Ukrainian troops left Avdiivka to avoid encirclement and withdrew to new defensive lines, Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said. “Based on the operational situation around Avdiivka, in order to avoid encirclement and preserve the lives and health of servicemen, I decided to withdraw our units from the city and transition to defense on more advantageous lines,” Syrskyi said.
messages.ru: Today in the Kremlin, Defense Minister S.K. Shoigu reported to the Supreme Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation on the full capture of the city of Avdiivka of the Donetsk People’s Republic, which is a defensive hub of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, under the control of the “Center” group of forces under the command of Colonel General Andrei Mordvichev.
messages.neutral: Russia took control of Avdiivka, while Ukrainian troops withdrew from the city.

messages.ua: War is about who killed whom and how many. In that sense, Avdiivka was very advantageous for us, said Yevhen Dykyi, former commander of an assault company of the Aidar battalion. He understands that saying this is absolutely inhumane because we also lost many people there. But by the orcs’ calculations, in the battles for Avdiivka they deployed more fighters than for Bakhmut.
messages.ru: The liberation of Avdiivka is necessary to push the front line away from Donetsk, and, most importantly, to protect it from terrorist attacks by the criminal Kyiv regime. Information about the troop advance was not made public until the complete defeat and capture of the city. The “Center” group’s troops continue offensive operations to liberate the Donetsk People’s Republic from Ukrainian nationalists.
messages.neutral: Heavy fighting took place in Avdiivka, resulting in significant losses on both sides.

messages.ua: GUR and the Air Force destroyed a Russian A-50 in the Sea of Azov area. And our correspondents from the scene report that two aircraft crashed. Orcs, happy holidays!
messages.ru: On the loss of a second A-50U of Russia’s Aerospace Forces and the painful problem of “friendly fire” from air defense. February 23, 2024 was marked by the loss of an A-50U airborne early warning aircraft. This is the second A-50U since the start of the year. Despite the fact that investigators are already working with the S-400 air defense crew that may have launched at the A-50U, as well as the command post, there is still no final clarity — only versions.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50U aircraft was destroyed over the Sea of Azov in February 2024.

messages.ua: GUR and the Air Force destroyed a Russian A-50 in the Sea of Azov area. And our correspondents from the scene report that two aircraft crashed. Orcs, happy holidays!
messages.ru: Russian air defense crews were misled by enemy missiles, and the hit on the A-50U was accidental. Technically, one cannot exclude the possibility of an intentional launch of surface-to-air missiles by the enemy (for example, a modified S-200 “Dubna”) toward our aircraft in order to provoke Russian air defense to return fire.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50 aircraft was destroyed over the Sea of Azov.

messages.ua: Minus an A-50U — GUR and the Air Force shot down another Russian aircraft, GUR says. As a result of a joint operation by the Main Intelligence Directorate of Ukraine’s Defense Ministry and the Air Force of the Armed Forces of Ukraine over the Sea of Azov, another valuable Russian A-50U aircraft was shot down. The cost of such an aircraft — of which the aggressor state has only a few left — is $350 million.
messages.ru: On the loss of a second A-50U of Russia’s Aerospace Forces and the painful problem of “friendly fire” from air defense. February 23, 2024 was marked by the loss of an A-50U airborne early warning aircraft. This is the second A-50U since the start of the year. Despite the fact that investigators are already working with the S-400 air defense crew that may have launched at the A-50U, as well as the command post, there is still no final clarity — only versions.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50U aircraft was destroyed over the Sea of Azov in February 2024.

messages.ua: Minus an A-50U — GUR and the Air Force shot down another Russian aircraft, GUR says. As a result of a joint operation by the Main Intelligence Directorate of Ukraine’s Defense Ministry and the Air Force of the Armed Forces of Ukraine over the Sea of Azov, another valuable Russian A-50U aircraft was shot down. The cost of such an aircraft — of which the aggressor state has only a few left — is $350 million.
messages.ru: Russian air defense crews were misled by enemy missiles, and the hit on the A-50U was accidental. Technically, one cannot exclude the possibility of an intentional launch of surface-to-air missiles by the enemy (for example, a modified S-200 “Dubna”) toward our aircraft in order to provoke Russian air defense to return fire.
messages.neutral: A Russian A-50U aircraft was downed over the Sea of Azov.

messages.ua: A brilliant operation, and this time “Sergey Kotov” was definitely destroyed, said GUR spokesperson Yusov. Earlier there had already been successful attacks on the ship, but it was repaired. “The enemy had plans for it. Besides the patrol ship itself, there were also plans to place air defense missile systems on the ship. There are dead and wounded. Part of the crew managed to evacuate, so the exact information is being clarified. At least 10 ambulances were called to the shore.”
messages.ru: At night, Ukrainian formations used missiles, UAVs, and naval drones (USVs) to try to attack targets in Crimea. One of the targets was the Crimean Bridge. The bridge is fine; the attack was repelled. However, things are not so good for the targets of the naval drones. As comrade Rybar rightly wrote, after failures at the front Ukraine bears large reputational risks and, as a rule, after each fiasco at the front they do something outrageous. Everyone knew — it was only a matter of waiting. And so it happened: the naval drones attacked the Project 22160 patrol ship “Sergey Kotov.” I am still clarifying the fate of the crew, but the vessel is done for.
messages.neutral: Ukrainian forces conducted attacks in Crimea, including a strike on the Russian patrol ship Sergey Kotov, with differing reports regarding damage and casualties.

messages.ua: 7 dead and 6 wounded — GUR calculated the occupiers’ losses as a result of the sinking of “Sergey Kotov.” The enemy likely managed to evacuate another 52 crew members who were on board. UPD: At least 27 crew members of the Russian ship “Sergey Kotov,” destroyed near the Kerch Strait, sustained injuries of varying severity, GUR clarified.
messages.ru: At night, Ukrainian formations used missiles, UAVs, and naval drones (USVs) to try to attack targets in Crimea. One of the targets was the Crimean Bridge. The bridge is fine; the attack was repelled. However, things are not so good for the targets of the naval drones. As comrade Rybar rightly wrote, after failures at the front Ukraine bears large reputational risks and, as a rule, after each fiasco at the front they do something outrageous. Everyone knew — it was only a matter of waiting. And so it happened: the naval drones attacked the Project 22160 patrol ship “Sergey Kotov.” I am still clarifying the fate of the crew, but the vessel is done for.
messages.neutral: Ukrainian forces carried out attacks in Crimea, including an operation targeting the Russian ship Sergey Kotov near the Kerch Strait, resulting in reported casualties.

messages.ua: In an evening address, President Zelenskyy once again reminded that Putin is a criminal and “must end up in the dock in The Hague.” “These days the Russian dictator is imitating another election. Everyone in the world understands that this figure, as has often happened in history, is simply sick with power and is doing everything to rule for life. For everything done by Russian killers in this war and for Putin’s lifelong power, there must be just retribution. He fears only one thing the most — justice. There is no legitimacy in this imitation of elections and cannot be. This figure must end up in the dock in The Hague — that is exactly what we must ensure. Everyone in the world who values life and decency,” Zelenskyy said.
messages.ru: The election results are the trust of the country’s citizens and their hope that we will do everything as planned. People came to the presidential election to create conditions for internal political consolidation. On challenges in the new term: we need to solve the tasks of the special military operation, strengthen defense capability and the Armed Forces. On the new cabinet: no need to fuss; there are two and a half months, we’ll think calmly. On the banned RDK: they are Vlasovites who fight on the side of Bandera followers. On Navalny’s death: that’s life, nothing can be done; he was ready to exchange him. Russia is for peace talks on Ukraine, but if they want to build relations seriously… On the possibility of joining Russia’s Kharkiv region: I do not exclude that Russia will be forced to create a sanitary zone in territories controlled by the Kyiv regime.
messages.neutral: Following Russia’s elections, President Zelensky criticized the legitimacy of the vote and called for justice over the war, while President Putin addressed domestic unity, military priorities, and potential future relations with Ukraine.

messages.ua: Russian “exit polls” have already drawn Putin a victory in the “elections” with more than 87% of the vote. The pseudo-elections in Russia have ended and the first “exit poll” results have already been published. Russians also report that turnout in the occupied part of Ukraine exceeded 80%. People there were forced to “vote.”
messages.ru: The elections showed the consolidation of society and the highest level of trust in Putin, Volodin said.
messages.neutral: Elections were held in Russia, with official reports indicating high voter turnout and strong results for President Vladimir Putin.

messages.ua: A Ukrainian in Amsterdam congratulated Russians on the elections with their favorite Russian dish — “pancakes on a shovel.” Observation 1 — interesting how many of them there are around the world. Observation 2 — these smiling faces still believe that elections in their terrorist country mean something. Observation 3 — of course, even the older ones didn’t understand the irony and enjoyed it with pleasure.
messages.ru: The elections showed the consolidation of society and the highest level of trust in Putin, Volodin said.
messages.neutral: Elections were held in Russia, resulting in a victory for President Vladimir Putin.

messages.ua: In Sevastopol, all services have been put on combat readiness; authorities speak of clumsy air defense operation. The governor of temporarily occupied Sevastopol said that in the inner harbor area, Sevastopol Bay, and South Bay, the military is using a smoke screen. Waiting for news about holes in Russia’s Black Sea Fleet.
messages.ru: The military is repelling a massive attack on Sevastopol; preliminarily, more than 10 missiles have already been shot down, the governor reported.
messages.neutral: Air defenses were activated in Sevastopol during a reported missile attack.

messages.ua: Three Russian air defense military units were attacked in Crimea this night, Russian media report. They say there were hits on facilities of the 31st Air Defense Division of the Russian Defense Ministry in Chornomorske and Saky districts. The Dzhankoy airfield was also attacked. Five soldiers were wounded.
messages.ru: UAF militants struck Crimea at night with ATACMS missiles; air defense systems worked over Dzhankoy and Simferopol, Rogov reported.
messages.neutral: Missile strikes targeted several locations in Crimea, including Dzhankoy, Simferopol, and Saky, with reports of Russian air defense activity.

messages.ua: Three Russian air defense military units were attacked in Crimea this night, Russian media report. They say there were hits on facilities of the 31st Air Defense Division of the Russian Defense Ministry in Chornomorske and Saky districts. The Dzhankoy airfield was also attacked. Five soldiers were wounded.
messages.ru: On a new missile strike on Crimea. Ukrainian formations are increasing the intensity of missile strikes on Crimea. Over a week and a half, attacks were carried out on Dzhankoy and Cape Tarkhankut, and tonight — on Simferopol and again on Dzhankoy. Over three missile attacks, more than 30 ATACMS operational-tactical missiles were launched. Their use was noted earlier too, but after the official announcement of a new aid package, the UAF uses only “ATACMS.” This night, the UAF launched 12 missiles (eight at the airfield in Dzhankoy, four at the airfield in Hvardiiske). Residents in nearby settlements heard explosions and air defense work. There is no precise information about the launch area, but with high probability the launch was from Kherson region (during the strike on Tarkhankut, launchers were in Beryslav).
messages.neutral: Missile strikes targeted several locations in Crimea, including Dzhankoy, Simferopol, and Saky, with reports of explosions.

messages.ua: In the Kharkiv direction, our soldiers are repelling an enemy attack toward Murom–Starytsia. Ukrainian units are making maximum efforts to strengthen defensive lines. According to updated information, over the past day the enemy lost more than 180 people in the Kharkiv direction, as well as 54 units of weapons and military equipment, including: two artillery systems destroyed and five damaged; 14 UAVs destroyed; 11 vehicles destroyed and 17 damaged; one tank damaged; one air defense asset damaged; three units of special equipment destroyed; and one enemy ammunition depot. The battle continues. Strength is in unity! Together we will win! Glory to Ukraine!
messages.ru: Statement by the head of the press center of the “North” group. Units of the “North” group, as a result of active actions, advanced deeper into the enemy’s defenses and inflicted losses on personnel and equipment of the UAF’s 3rd Tank and 57th Motorized Infantry Brigades in the areas of Vovchanski Khutory, Bilyi Kolodiaz, and Petropavlivka in Kharkiv region. Three counterattacks by enemy assault groups were repelled near Vovchansk and Lyptsi in Kharkiv region. UAF losses over the day amounted to up to 255 servicemen and seven vehicles, a 122-mm Grad MLRS combat vehicle, a 152-mm Akatsiya self-propelled howitzer, and a 155-mm M777 howitzer (foreign-made).
messages.neutral: Fighting continued in the Kharkiv region near Vovchansk, Liptsy, and other settlements, with both sides reporting heavy clashes and losses.

messages.ua: In the Kharkiv direction, the enemy continues to use guided aerial bombs against Ukrainian settlements from the territory of the Russian Federation. Three more KABs were dropped by terrorists from the direction of Russia’s Belgorod onto Bilyi Kolodiaz. In total, since the start of the day, this village has been hit by six KAB airstrikes from Russian airspace.
messages.ru: Units of the “West” group over the week liberated the settlements of Berestove and Ivanivka in Kharkiv region and continued advancing deeper into the enemy’s defenses. They inflicted losses on formations of eight UAF units and two National Guard brigades of Ukraine. They repelled 11 counterattacks by enemy units. UAF losses amounted to up to 2310 servicemen, three tanks, 10 armored combat vehicles, 35 vehicles, two 122-mm Bastion MLRS combat vehicles, as well as 30 field artillery guns, including 14 Western-made.
messages.neutral: Fighting intensified in the Kharkiv region, with reports of casualties on both sides and Russian aerial bomb attacks.

messages.ua: Rustem Umerov: A massive missile strike on Ukraine by the Russian terrorist state. The missile attack was carried out during rush hour, when Ukrainians were going to work. This time, the targets of dozens of Kalibr and Kinzhal missiles launched from Volgograd region included not only civilian and residential infrastructure. The enemy hit the National Children’s Specialized Hospital “Okhmatdyt” with its so-called “high-precision” weapons. Dozens killed and wounded, including children… This crime by Russia is being documented and will be submitted to international institutions for a fair investigation as a crime against civilians. Our defensive capabilities are still insufficient. We need rapid decisions from allies on providing additional weapons to protect the sky and civilians. We need more air defense systems. We continue working to ensure that the promised systems arrive in Ukraine as quickly as possible. We are seeking opportunities to attract additional systems. We will stand and we will win.
messages.ru: Missile shelling of Kyiv hospital: the Kremlin blames the Ukrainian army.
messages.neutral: A missile attack struck the Okhmatdyt children’s hospital in Kyiv.

messages.ua: The Okhmatdyt children’s hospital in Kyiv. One of the most important not only in Ukraine but also in Europe — exclusively a CHILDREN’S hospital. Okhmatdyt has saved and restored the health of thousands of children. Now the hospital is damaged by a Russian strike; people are under the rubble; the exact number of wounded and killed is currently unknown. Everyone is helping to clear the rubble: doctors and ordinary people. Russia cannot not know where its missiles are flying, and must fully answer for all its crimes: against people, against children, against humanity as a whole. It is very important that the world does not remain silent now and that everyone sees what Russia is and what it is doing.
messages.ru: Missile shelling of Kyiv hospital: the Kremlin blames the Ukrainian army.
messages.neutral: A missile attack struck the Okhmatdyt children’s hospital in Kyiv.

messages.ua: The Pentagon published the full list of what will be included in the new U.S. aid package for $225 million: Patriot air defense batteries; ammunition for NASAMS and HIMARS; anti-aircraft missiles for Stinger; 155 mm and 105 mm artillery shells; TOW anti-tank guided missiles; Javelin and AT-4 anti-tank systems; small-arms ammunition; explosive munitions.
messages.ru: The U.S. announced a new $225 million aid package for Ukraine, the Pentagon reported. Blinken added that as part of it Washington will provide Kyiv with a Patriot air defense battery. Total U.S. military support for Ukraine during Biden’s presidency exceeded $54.4 billion, the Pentagon said. Germany, the U.S., France and Britain are directly participating in the conflict around Ukraine, Peskov told Pavel Zarubin. According to him, all the attributes of the Cold War — with confrontation and direct opposition — are returning in relations with the West. Peskov added that Russia must take current Western decisions into account; this is not a reason for pessimism, but rather a reason to pull together and use the full potential.
messages.neutral: The United States announced a new $225 million military aid package for Ukraine, including Patriot air defense systems and various types of ammunition.

messages.ua: The U.S. and Ukraine are working on a strong response to the Russian strike on Okhmatdyt, Biden’s adviser said. “We are working to counter this attack and respond to it with force,” Sullivan said.
messages.ru: The U.S. believes Ukraine must itself be responsible for air defense and protecting the skies, without counting on the involvement of third-country forces, the White House said.
messages.neutral: U.S. officials discussed a response to a Russian strike on Ukraine.

messages.ua: The occupiers blew up the Kakhovka HPP, an evacuation began.
messages.ru: Special military operation, June 6: a terrorist attack at the Kakhovka HPP.
messages.neutral: On June 6, an explosion occurred at the Kakhovka hydroelectric power station.

messages.ua: Operational Command 'South' reported that the Kakhovka HPP was blown up by Russian occupying troops; later the Regional Military Administration confirmed this information.
messages.ru: Last night, Ukrainian troops carried out a terrorist attack at the Kakhovka HPP, as a result populated areas in the Kherson region were flooded. The terrorist attack at the Kakhovka HPP is a large-scale sabotage operation planned in advance by Kyiv, which the Armed Forces of Ukraine carried out in order to redeploy troops to the area of their offensive actions from the Kherson direction, weakening their positions there, Shoigu said.
messages.neutral: An explosion occurred at the Kakhovka hydroelectric power station.

messages.ua: The Grey Zone Telegram channel, close to the Wagner PMC, claims that Yevgeny Prigozhin's plane was shot down by air defense fire. They also write that a terrorist attack on board is being considered as a version of the Prigozhin plane crash.
messages.ru: In the Kremlin they called to "base it on facts" in the story with the plane crash. Commenting on statements voiced in the West about the Kremlin's involvement in the incident, Dmitry Peskov said that "all of this is absolutely a lie." "Now, of course, around this air disaster, around the tragic death of the plane's passengers, including Yevgeny Prigozhin, there is a lot of speculation. Of course, in the West all these speculations are presented from a known angle," the presidential press secretary said.
messages.neutral: A plane carrying Yevgeny Prigozhin crashed, resulting in the deaths of those on board.

messages.ua: The attack on the Crimean Bridge, which took place on the night of July 17, was a special operation by the SBU and the Naval Forces of Ukraine. The overpass built by the occupiers was attacked with surface drones. That night, explosions were heard in the area of the bridge. According to data from Russian sources, one span of the bridge was destroyed, another shifted. Traffic was blocked.
messages.ru: On the night of July 17, the Crimean Bridge in the area of the 145th support was attacked by Ukrainian neo-Nazis. The head of Crimea, Sergey Aksyonov, stated that an incident occurred because of which vehicle traffic across the bridge was completely stopped, but he did not provide details of the incident.
messages.neutral: On the night of July 17, explosions occurred near the Crimean Bridge, damaging parts of the structure and halting traffic.

messages.ua: NATO summit: we explain what exactly Ukraine received in Vilnius. Ukraine did not receive an official invitation to membership in NATO at the summit in Vilnius, but it obtained a simplified accession procedure and assurances from leaders that it will become a member of the Alliance after victory.
messages.ru: Expectations for the latter from the summit turned out to be overstated. On July 11, the President of Ukraine Volodymyr Zelensky sharply condemned the plans of Western politicians not to include any specifics on Kyiv's membership in the alliance in the statement being prepared. "Uncertainty is weakness," Zelensky said, adding that he would discuss this "frankly" at the summit. It is noteworthy that a month earlier he had threatened not to come to the meeting in Lithuania if it did not give a "signal" about the country's accession to the alliance. However, on July 9, US President Joe Biden directly stated that Ukraine is not yet ready for NATO membership. Thus, in the opinion of Ivan Skorikov, head of the Ukraine department at the Institute of CIS Countries, Zelensky went to the summit in Vilnius essentially with nothing. The West, he said, realizes that inviting Kyiv into the alliance is fraught with a direct clash with Russia and this could agitate Western public opinion.
messages.neutral: At the NATO summit in Vilnius, Ukraine did not receive an official invitation to join NATO.

messages.ua: The Crimean Bridge is partially destroyed.
messages.ru: The terrorist attack on the Crimean Bridge is a reaction of Ukrainian embassies. As Rybar writes, at the moment when debris on the bridge were being driven past by cars, therefore "with a high degree of probability" their drivers and passengers. "Responsibility for the targeted killing of persons." However, the world public will definitely not have such facts, so Ukrainian embassies around the world feel free and celebrate the terrorist attack in their social networks.
messages.neutral: The Crimean Bridge was partially destroyed following an explosion.
